feed.title: Thys
feed.subtitle: A minimal, responsive, and powerful Jekyll theme for presenting professional writing.

entry.title: Getting Started with Deneb
entry.summary:
What is Deneb?
As quoted on Deneb’s site, Deneb is … a certified custom visual for Microsoft Power BI….
It’s developed by Daniel Marsh-Patrick. The custom visual is build on Vega and Vega-lite visual language.
Please visit the Deneb website. Here you will find more information on how to get started with this custom visual.
Why do I use Deneb?
Deneb is a lot of fun to work with. I love to ...

entry.title: The one with the bar chart.

link.title: The one with the bar chart.

entry.summary:
The chart was posted as part of the Workout Wednesday PowerBI challenge. I am only going to provide the specification and configuration for the Cost Group bar chart. The others is similar in their approach.
  Dataset The dataset can be found on the Workout Wednesday link above.
  Additional fields created
Actual CM Actual CM = VAR __previousMonth = CALCULATE ( ...

entry.title: The one with the card visual.

link.title: The one with the card visual.

entry.summary:
The chart was posted as part of the Workout Wednesday PowerBI challenge. I am only going to provide the specification and configuration for the Actual Spend card visual. The others is similar in their approach.

entry.title: The one with the matrix.
entry.summary:
The chart was posted as part of the Tableau Workout Wednesday 2018 challenge.
I was not able to add the GRAND TOTAL column to the matrix, maybe someone can provide a solution to that.
  Dataset The dataset can be found on the Workout Wednesday link above. Added a calculated table
Date Date = VAR __StartYear = YEAR ( MIN ( Orders[Order Date] ) ) VAR __EndYear = YEAR ( MA...

entry.title: The one with the dumbbell chart.

link.title: The one with the dumbbell chart.

entry.summary:
The chart was posted as part of the Tableau Workout Wednesday 2018 challenge.
  Dataset The dataset can be found on the Workout Wednesday link above.
  Additional fields created
Sales Sales = SUM ( 'Orders'[Sales] )
  Fields used
Category Sub-Category Region Sales
  Specification
